title: Triclisia gilletii

This family is part of the larger order Ranunculales, which includes over 200 species of flowering plants. Triclisia gilletii is further classified as a genus within the family Menispermaceae, and it is native to tropical Africa. intro: Triclisia gilletii, also known as the African bush mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Triclisia gilletii include triclisine, glycosides, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In traditional medicine, Triclisia gilletii is typically used to prepare herbal decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions.
